title: Weinmannia icacifolia
intro: Weinmannia icacifolia, also known as Miro, is a tree species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Cunoniaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Weinmannia icacifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Maori medicine, Weinmannia icacifolia is often prepared as a tea or infused in water to create a medicinal decoction, or it is made into a poultice to apply topically for wound healing and pain relief.
